prompt_text: Planhat vs Gainsight for enterprise SaaS CS and RevOps teams managing complex customer portfolios
response:
# Planhat vs Gainsight: Enterprise SaaS Comparison

## Quick Verdict
- **Planhat**: Better for CS-first teams prioritizing operational efficiency and customer journey mapping
- **Gainsight**: Better for RevOps-heavy orgs needing deeper revenue intelligence and cross-functional alignment

## Head-to-Head Breakdown

### Data Architecture & Integration

| Aspect | Planhat | Gainsight |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| **Data Model** | Customer-centric, flatter structure | Account-centric with revenue focus |
| **Native Integrations** | ~150 (strong with product analytics) | ~400+ (Salesforce-first) |
| **Custom Objects** | Limited | Extensive (Relationship Intelligence objects) |
| **Salesforce Sync** | Good but optional | Extremely tight (Salesforce-native feel) |

**Winner for complex portfolios**: Gainsight (if Salesforce-heavy); Planhat (if tech-agnostic)

---

### Customer Health Scoring

**Planhat**
- Simple, transparent rule builder
- Health cards visible in customer timeline
- Good for operational hygiene tracking
- Struggles with multi-dimensional revenue scenarios

**Gainsight**
- AI-powered predictive health scoring (Gainsight Einstein)
- Relationship Intelligence surfaces hidden risks
- Maps to revenue impact directly
- Steeper learning curve

**Winner**: Gainsight for RevOps; Planhat for CS operations

---

### Journey Mapping & Execution

**Planhat**
- Visual journey builder with stage gating
- Excellent for CSM playbook execution
- Campaign-style orchestration (better than most)
- Task automation tied to milestones
- More intuitive for non-technical users

**Gainsight**
- Playbooks engine (action-oriented)
- Program management layer
- Less "journey" visualization, more "intervention"
- Requires more configuration

**Winner**: Planhat (clearer visual model and execution)

---

### Revenue Operations Capabilities

| Feature | Planhat | Gainsight |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| **Upsell/Cross-sell Guidance** | Basic | Advanced (revenue intelligence) |
| **Churn Prediction** | Reactive | Proactive & predictive |
| **Territory Management** | No | Yes |
| **Pricing Intelligence** | Limited | Competitive benchmarking |
| **ARR Tracking** | Basic | Native (deeply integrated) |

**Winner**: Gainsight (dominant for RevOps motion)

---

### Ease of Use & Implementation

**Planhat**
- 6-12 week implementation (typical)
- Cleaner UI/UX
- Faster time to value
- Lower learning curve
- Smaller admin team sufficient

**Gainsight**
- 12-20 weeks (complex enterprises)
- Requires Salesforce expertise
- Powerful but configuration-heavy
- Dedicated admin role needed
- Steeper learning curve

**Winner**: Planhat (faster, simpler)

---

### Reporting & Analytics

**Planhat**
- KPI dashboards (CS-focused)
- Cohort analysis solid
- Timeline-based insights
- Export-friendly

**Gainsight**
- Advanced analytics engine
- Revenue waterfall reporting
- Forecasting capabilities
- Executive dashboard templates
- Relationship Intelligence insights

**Winner**: Gainsight (deeper, more executive-ready)

---

## Decision Matrix

### Choose **Planhat** if:
- ✅ CS operations excellence is priority #1
- ✅ You want fast deployment & ROI
- ✅ Tech stack is diverse (not Salesforce-centric)
- ✅ Team size is 5-20 CSMs
- ✅ Customer health/lifecycle is main use case
- ✅ Budget constraint is real (~$50-100K/year)

### Choose **Gainsight** if:
- ✅ Revenue operations alignment is critical
- ✅ Salesforce is your system of record
- ✅ Need predictive/AI-driven insights
- ✅ Complex deal desk/upsell motions
- ✅ Large enterprises (100+ customers)
- ✅ Budget $100-300K+/year
- ✅ Executive visibility into
